bird.name: White-tailed swallow
bird.summary: The white-tailed swallow (Hirundo megaensis) is a small swallow belonging to the family Hirundinidae and is endemic to Oromia, Ethiopia. It…

relations.subject: White-tailed swallow
relations.evidence: The natural habitat of the white-tailed swallow is subtropical or tropical high-altitude shrubland It is endemic to southern Ethiopia and particularly restricted to the Yabelo–Mega region of Oromia.{{cite journal|author=Mellanby, R.J.|author2= Ross, B.|author3= Watt, A.|author4= Wondafrash, M.|author5= Ewnetu, M.|author6= Broadhurst, C.|author7= Spottiswoode, C
relations.object_summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

relations.subject: White-tailed swallow
relations.evidence: |year=2008|title= Distribution, abundance and habitat preferences of White-tailed Swallow Hirundo megaensis and Ethiopian Bush-crow Zavattariornis stresemanni, two southern Ethiopian endemics|journal=Bird Conservation International|volume=18|issue= 4|pages= 395–412|doi= 10.1017/S0959270908007314}} It is found between altitudes of ; however, it is not prevalent in altitudes lower than . The white-tailed swallow is most commonly found in both savanna and grassland pastures of high elevation.{{cite journal|author=Spottiswoode, C.N.|author2= Wondafrash, M.|author3= Gabremichael, M
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: White-tailed swallow
relations.evidence: It is one of the most threatened bird species by climate change and a massive range reduction is projected in the future
relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。
